basics.name: Usman Ayobami
basics.label: Software Engineer
basics.summary: Product-focused software engineer with strong fundamentals in algorithms, data structures, and full-stack web development. 2+ years of experience building and shipping features using React, TypeScript, Node.js, and Postgres. Comfortable working across the stack, debugging production issues, and owning features end-to-end in fast-paced, high-stakes environments.

work.name: Daylight Health
highlights: Built patient-facing and internal features using React, TypeScript, GraphQL, and Node.js.
Designed and shipped insurance-verification flows that reduced clinic workload by 40%.
Delivered a Next.js-based file upload interface with OCR extraction and real-time validation.
Used AI tools (Cursor, GPT-based refactoring) to accelerate development and improve quality.

work.name: Meeting Detection Engine
highlights: Created a cross-platform meeting detection engine with a hybrid signal system.
Integrated Rust bindings with napi-rs and exposed a clean TypeScript API.
Published as a npm package with full documentation and real-world adoption.

work.name: Michigan Geological Survey
highlights: Built interactive dashboards using React and Python for carbon sequestration research.
Improved large dataset querying speed by 30% with optimized data pipelines.

work.name: PanAfrican Capital Holdings
highlights: Developed scalable front-end systems with TypeScript, Redux, and CSS.
Contributed to business-aligned, production-ready software solutions.

work.name: Genesys Tech Hub
highlights: Built responsive web and mobile apps using React Native, JavaScript, and MongoDB.
Collaborated on REST API integrations and frontend architecture.

projects.name: HealthDisparity AI
projects.description: Built an AI assistant recommending culturally competent care using React, Node.js, OpenAI.
highlights: Designed end-to-end products with analytics and clean UX.

projects.name: Core Extract
projects.description: Developed a reliable AI pipeline to extract structured data from PDFs using Python, Rust, and OCR.
highlights: Focused on accuracy and robust failure handling.
